title: Beta-Defensive Continuation
hypothesis: A more defensive beta-vol-inverse branch can test whether the broad momentum family still has a viable path when gross exposure, deployment speed, and position sizing are all tightened together.
why_different_from_retired: The retired broad momentum work exhausted raw broad, risk-sector, and drawdown-guarded paths, while this branch stays beta-aware and explicitly lowers gross exposure, initial deployment, and position concentration at the same time.
This is not a re-enable of a retired queue item; it is a repo-backed but previously ungoverned continuation branch that changes the basket-construction risk posture materially.
success_criteria: The beta-defensive seed must pass the existing validation, holdout, and walk-forward promotion policy without exceptions before it can move toward paper-trade preparation.
If it fails, it must still produce enough evidence to decide whether defensive beta-aware momentum deserves any further backlog space.

stop_conditions.when: The beta-defensive seed passes the existing promotion policy and remains acceptable for downstream paper-trading preparation.
The beta-defensive seed records a terminal promotion decision and still fails the policy, especially on validation, holdout, or walk-forward thresholds.

bootstrap.objective: Test whether a more defensive beta-vol-inverse branch can improve late-period benchmark-relative robustness without reopening the retired risk + sector family.
bootstrap.branch_rationale: The broad beta and drawdown branches have already exhausted their defined operability paths, and the risk + sector branch is explicitly retired. The unused beta-defensive seed is the cleanest repo-backed materially different hypothesis already encoded in config.
positive_hypotheses: Beta-vol-inverse weighting with lower gross exposure, tighter deployment, and smaller max weights is a materially different robustness hypothesis than the exhausted broad and drawdown seeds.
A defensive branch can target the late-period benchmark-relative holdout problem more directly than more local ranking or regime tweaks on the retired family.

seed_stack.label: Broad beta control
seed_stack.rationale: Reference broad candidate family and the original control for the branch that has already exhausted its queued operability work.

seed_stack.label: Beta-defensive seed
seed_stack.rationale: Repo-defined defensive follow-up using beta-vol-inverse weighting, lower gross exposure, lower initial deployment, and smaller position caps.

campaign_path.label: Beta-Defensive Seed
campaign_path.purpose: Run the next materially different broad-family hypothesis and decide whether it deserves further follow-up or retirement.
